metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add outbound_differ.py and run_differs.py to Scope and describe the wiring seam: how a SupportsLinks capability (or the canonical link set) is threaded run_differs -> compute_outbound_mutations -> _compute_outbound_update_mutation -> _diff_links (mirroring 625b's inbound_mapper=backend.inbound), and that the _existing_jira_links re-export at outbound_differ.py:71-73 is removed.
evidence: Plan Approach: 'Core _diff_links/_diff_link_removals ... then compare local ticket["deps"] against the canonical set in relation vocabulary' — the canonical set is produced by SupportsLinks.map_remote_links, a backend capability.

outbound_differ.py:774 currently calls `_diff_links(ticket, jira_fields, binding_store)` with the RAW vendor snapshot; compute_outbound_mutations (line 396) and _compute_outbound_update_mutation (line 652) thread only outbound_mapper/inbound_mapper — no SupportsLinks object.
run_differs.py:497-512 (production) passes `outbound_mapper=backend.outbound, inbound_mapper=backend.inbound` but no link capability, though the SupportsLinks-capable `backend` object is in scope.
outbound_differ.py:71-73 re-exports `_existing_jira_links` — a symbol the plan moves adapter-side.

Sibling child 625b (identical field-diff canonicalization) DOES enumerate outbound_differ.py + run_differs.py in its Scope; 625b's remediation note 2 explicitly states 'Links are OUT of 625b's code scope ... 625b makes no change to _diff_links'. Epic bbf1 Success Criteria requires disjoint children each owning their core modules — so the link-path wiring is owned by neither child as written.
findings.finding: The plan relocates _existing_jira_links + relation translation adapter-side and has core _diff_links compare against a canonical set from SupportsLinks.map_remote_links, but its Scope omits outbound_differ.py and run_differs.py — the sites that must be edited to obtain the SupportsLinks capability and thread it (plus the canonical set) into _diff_links, and to drop outbound_differ.py's _existing_jira_links re-export.
scenarios: Executor implements map_remote_links/link_payload_for_relation but has no code path connecting them to outbound_links._diff_links, since the only differ call site and its production injection point are out of the declared edit-set.
The _existing_jira_links re-export at outbound_differ.py:73 breaks when the symbol is moved adapter-side, but outbound_differ.py is not listed for edit.
findings.suggested_fix: Mirror sibling 625b: thread the SupportsLinks capability from run_differs (backend is already in scope) into compute_outbound_mutations as a keyword param, forward it through _compute_outbound_update_mutation to _diff_links/_diff_link_removals, and default it to None resolving via the neutral select_backend seam for direct-call test sites; drop the _existing_jira_links re-export.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how `map_remote_links` preserves direction-agnostic ADD dedup: either (a) return BOTH canonical directions for each asymmetric link (e.g. both `("blocks", Y)` and `("depends_on", Y)` for any `Blocks` link to Y), or (b) keep the ADD dedup key in vendor-type space (using `opaque_vendor_type` + `remote_key`) rather than canonical-relation space.
evidence: Plan: 'The Jira implementation absorbs today's `_existing_jira_links` (`outbound_links.py:38-63`) including its direction-agnostic dedup (`:57-62`)'
Plan: 'map_remote_links(remote_fields) → the canonical link set: entries of (relation, remote_key, opaque_vendor_type)'
outbound_links.py:57-62: iterates both 'inwardIssue' and 'outwardIssue', adding (type_name, side_key_val) for each — one vendor-typed entry per direction, direction-agnostic by key design
outbound_links.py:116: dedup check `if key in existing` where key = (jira_type, target_key) — vendor-type-named, hits regardless of direction
link_direction.py:43-46: resolve_inbound_link returns ONE canonical relation per link (outward→blocks, inward→depends_on) — so each Blocks link becomes exactly one canonical entry, not two
findings.finding: The proposed `map_remote_links` mechanism loses the existing direction-agnostic ADD dedup. The current `_existing_jira_links` inserts `(jira_type_name, other_key)` for BOTH `inwardIssue` and `outwardIssue` sides (lines 57-62), so any `Blocks` link to key Y appears as `("Blocks", Y)` regardless of which endpoint the local issue occupies. The refactored ADD dedup would compare a local `"blocks"` dep against a canonical set where the same physical Jira link appears as EITHER `("blocks", Y)` OR `("depends_on", Y)` — not both — so a local `blocks Y` dep will fail the dedup check when the Jira link was recorded in the opposite direction (`inwardIssue`), emitting a spurious ADD every pass.
scenarios: Jira has a Blocks link where local issue X is the inwardIssue (Y blocks X). The canonical set from map_remote_links contains ("depends_on", Y). Local dep says blocks=Y. link_payload_for_relation("blocks") → ("Blocks", False). ADD dedup checks for ("blocks", Y) — not found — emits spurious ADD every reconciler pass.
Any asymmetric relation (blocks/depends_on) whose Jira-side direction differs from the local dep's canonical direction will bypass dedup.
findings.suggested_fix: In the `map_remote_links` spec, state explicitly: for each Jira issuelink of a mapped asymmetric type (e.g. `Blocks`), insert entries for BOTH canonical relations against the same `remote_key` — i.e. both `("blocks", Y, "Blocks")` and `("depends_on", Y, "Blocks")`. The `_diff_links` ADD dedup then checks `(relation, target_key)` against this set and correctly matches regardless of which end the local issue occupies. The REMOVE path is unaffected as it already uses `resolve_inbound_link`'s single directional result for managed-ref matching.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the existing link-behavior test consumers the refactor touches (test_link_dedup.py, test_link_removal_sync.py, integration/test_link_sync.py, jira_contract/test_roundtrip.py, external/test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py, tests/scripts/reducer/test_managed_refs.py) and state how each direct _diff_links / raw-issuelinks compute_outbound_mutations call is migrated or preserved (including the direction-agnostic dedup case).
evidence: test_link_dedup.py calls `outbound_differ._diff_links(ticket, jira_fields, binding)` directly (lines 98/109/132/166) and test_outbound_diff_links_dedup_is_direction_agnostic (line 157) feeds an inward Blocks and asserts []; test_outbound_diff_links_maps_relation_type_and_swap asserts out[0]['swap'] over the full relation map.

test_link_removal_sync.py + test_managed_refs.py + integration/test_link_sync.py + jira_contract/test_roundtrip.py drive compute_outbound_mutations with raw `{'issuelinks': [...]}` snapshots and assert REMOVE/ADD link behavior.
tests/external/test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py:498 calls `outbound._diff_links(ticket, {"issuelinks": client.get_issue_links(key_a)}, bind)` directly.
Plan Scope lists only a generic 'tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/ (link-diff behavior tests)' bucket and the neutrality gate.
findings.finding: The refactor changes _diff_links's input from a raw vendor snapshot to a canonical set (and correspondingly the outbound link path's wiring), but the plan does not enumerate the existing behavior tests that call _diff_links / compute_outbound_mutations with raw vendor {issuelinks: ...} dicts and assert the type/swap orientation and direction-agnostic dedup.
scenarios: _diff_links's signature/input shape changes to a canonical set, breaking direct-call tests (test_link_dedup.py, test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py:498) not covered by the Scope's generic bucket.

compute_outbound_mutations link path now needs a SupportsLinks/canonical-set seam; integration/external/reducer tests that pass raw issuelinks without a backend must exercise a default-resolution fallback the plan doesn't mention.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'code review' in AC2's verification with a deterministic proving command (e.g. a grep or AST assertion that no string literal matching a Jira link-type name — e.g. `Blocks`, `Cloners`, `Duplicate` — appears in `outbound_links.py` outside of string-passthrough contexts, or extend the neutrality gate to assert no known vendor link-type literal in core).
evidence: 'verified by code review + the behavior tests in Testing (identical ADD/REMOVE payloads to today)'
No grep, AST, or import-scan command is cited to confirm the structural claim that vendor link-type names are opaque in core
AC1 already sets the precedent for an AST gate on import presence — the same approach is applicable here for the opaque-passenger claim
findings.finding: AC2's verification includes 'code review' as a co-equal proving method alongside behavior tests, but 'code review' is not a deterministic, repeatable proving command — it cannot be executed by CI and cannot produce binary pass/fail evidence.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC2: 'Link diffing compares canonical relations only… verified by code review + the behavior tests'
scenarios: An executor completes the behavior tests and considers AC2 done; a future change reintroduces a vendor type-name comparison in core that is not caught by the behavior tests alone, since the behavior tests only validate payload identity, not the internal comparison vocabulary
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Testing section or AC2: 'The AST neutrality gate is extended to assert that `outbound_links.py` contains no reference to known Jira link-type name literals (e.g. `Blocks`, `Cloners`) outside of opaque-passthrough contexts — satisfying AC2 mechanically alongside AC1.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test case (and specify the expected return value/behavior) for `link_payload_for_relation` when called with an unknown or unmapped relation — document whether it returns `None`, raises, or is guaranteed never called with an unknown value.
evidence: Testing section covers unknown *remote* link types (map_remote_links path) but is silent on unknown *local* relation passed to link_payload_for_relation
The inline replacement at outbound_links.py:105 uses .get(relation) which returns None; the new method's contract for an unknown relation is unspecified and untested
New method has at least two branches: known relation → (vendor_type, swap); unknown/None relation → ?
findings.finding: `link_payload_for_relation(relation)` is a new method with conditional logic (known vs. unknown relation), but the testing section does not specify what happens when a local dep carries a relation that has no mapping — the failure/invalid-input path for this new translator is absent from the test plan.
scenarios: A local ticket dep has a relation string not present in the adapter map (e.g. a relation added to core before the adapter is updated); link_payload_for_relation is called with it; the behavior (None return, KeyError, silent skip) is untested and unspecified, risking a silent wrong emission or an unhandled exception in the diff loop
findings.suggested_fix: In the Testing section, add: 'Unknown relation passed to link_payload_for_relation returns None (or raises a documented exception); outbound_links.py guards against None before building the ADD payload, mirroring today's .get() guard.' Then add a unit test asserting the adapter returns None for an unmapped relation and that the diff loop skips the emission.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state whether `adapters/jira/backend.py` is the sole implementor of `SupportsLinks` (and update any test doubles/stubs in Scope if not), and add an expand-contract note or rollback path for the interface addition.
evidence: Scope lists only `adapters/jira/backend.py` as implementing the two new members
No statement that this is the sole implementor of `SupportsLinks`
No acknowledgement of test doubles or other adapters that may implement `SupportsLinks`
No expand-contract sequence or rollback path described for the interface change
T4 requires: 'A change/removal consumers rely on is acknowledged with expand-contract or a rollback path'
findings.finding: Adding `map_remote_links` and `link_payload_for_relation` as new required members to `SupportsLinks` is a breaking interface change for any existing implementors beyond `adapters/jira/backend.py` (e.g. test doubles, stub adapters, future backends). The plan lists only the Jira adapter in Scope and does not acknowledge whether other implementors exist, whether test doubles need updating, or provide an expand-contract sequence or rollback path for the interface addition.
scenarios: A test double implementing `SupportsLinks` fails `make typecheck` or at runtime when the new required methods are absent
A second backend adapter (if any) silently breaks
The implementer updates only the Jira adapter, discovers broken test doubles only at CI, with no rollback guidance
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence in Approach or Scope: 'The Jira adapter is the sole production implementor of `SupportsLinks`; any test doubles implementing the capability are updated in the same PR. Because Python Protocols fail at typecheck rather than silently, `make typecheck` (AC3) serves as the completeness gate — no separate expand-contract phase is required.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a doc update to the Scope: either amend ADR 0035 with the two new `SupportsLinks` members (`map_remote_links`, `link_payload_for_relation`) and their contracts, or update `AGENTS.md` / the adapter-authoring guide so future adapter implementers know what to supply.
evidence: Plan adds `map_remote_links` and `link_payload_for_relation` to SupportsLinks with no mention of updating AGENTS.md or ADR 0035
ADR 0035 §(d) is cited as governing rationale but not listed as a file to update in Scope
Absence: no doc/AGENTS.md update in the Scope file list
findings.finding: The plan adds two new required members (`map_remote_links`, `link_payload_for_relation`) to the `SupportsLinks` capability protocol, creating new implementer obligations for any future adapter author, but no update to `AGENTS.md`, the ADR 0035 addendum, or an adapter-authoring guide is planned to document these obligations.
scenarios: A developer writing a second (non-Jira) adapter consults AGENTS.md or ADR 0035 and finds no mention of the two new required SupportsLinks members, leading to an incomplete adapter and a runtime AttributeError
ADR 0035 §(d) becomes partially stale: it describes the canonical-shape principle but not the specific protocol surface that implements it for link diffing
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: `docs/adr/0035-*.md` (or `AGENTS.md`) — append a paragraph under the existing §(d) entry describing the two new `SupportsLinks` members, their signatures, and the `opaque_vendor_type` passenger contract. This keeps the ADR as the single authoritative record of the canonical-boundary pattern for links.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add FakeBackend stub implementations of `map_remote_links` and `link_payload_for_relation` (satisfying the widened `SupportsLinks` Protocol) and extend the S2 contract suite to cover both new members — matching the pattern established by sibling 97f2.
evidence: Leaf scope lists: '_backend.py (SupportsLinks: map_remote_links + link_payload_for_relation)', 'adapters/jira/backend.py (implement the two members)' — FakeBackend and S2 contract suite are absent.
Sibling 97f2 scope explicitly includes: 'tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/ (S2 contract suite + FakeBackend updated to the widened Protocol)'
Parent bbf1 AC5: 'make lint && make typecheck && make test green; each landed with LLM-Review +1 and Verified +1'

Parent decomposition note: 'shared _backend.py/adapters/jira/backend.py edits are additive per child and recorded in each child's file impact' — does not exempt FakeBackend/contract-suite updates from widening children
findings.finding: The leaf widens the SupportsLinks Protocol on _backend.py with two new members (map_remote_links, link_payload_for_relation) but does not include updates to FakeBackend or the S2 contract suite in its scope or testing. The parent's structural rule — visible in sibling 97f2's explicit scope ('S2 contract suite + FakeBackend updated to the widened Protocol') and parent AC5 (make typecheck green) — requires every Protocol-widening change to keep all in-tree implementors in sync. Omitting these means FakeBackend will not satisfy the widened SupportsLinks Protocol, causing a typecheck failure that directly contradicts parent AC5.
findings.location: Leaf Scope section; parent bbf1 AC5 and child decomposition note 'shared _backend.py edits are additive per child'
scenarios: typecheck run fails because FakeBackend does not implement the new map_remote_links / link_payload_for_relation members required by SupportsLinks Protocol
S2 contract suite does not exercise the new capability members, leaving the port contract untested against the canonical implementation
findings.suggested_fix: Add `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/` (S2 contract suite + FakeBackend updated for the two new SupportsLinks members) to the leaf's Scope section and Testing section, mirroring sibling 97f2's scope entry for the same files.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify why `map_remote_links` and `link_payload_for_relation` belong on `SupportsLinks` (transport capability) rather than on `InboundMapper`/`OutboundMapper` (translation role Protocols) as the sibling 4af8/625b prior art establishes — or move them to the role Protocols for consistency.
evidence: Plan: 'Move both link translations onto the SupportsLinks capability (adapter-side, translation only)'
Existing SupportsLinks (_backend.py:199-210) holds only transport methods: set_relationship, get_issuelinks_map
Sibling prior art (story 4af8/625b): map_remote_to_local on InboundMapper, map_fields_to_remote on OutboundMapper — translation methods go on role Protocols, not capability Protocols
ABSENCE: plan gives no rationale for why SupportsLinks rather than InboundMapper/OutboundMapper
findings.finding: The plan adds translation methods (map_remote_links, link_payload_for_relation) to the SupportsLinks transport-capability Protocol, deviating from the project's established pattern (stories 4af8/625b) of placing translation methods on the dedicated role Protocols (InboundMapper / OutboundMapper) and leaving capability Protocols for transport-facing methods only. This mixes roles (translation vs. transport) on a single Protocol without justification, creating a precedent a second backend would have to follow.
findings.location: Approach section — 'Move both link translations onto the SupportsLinks capability'; _backend.py SupportsLinks (lines 199–210)
scenarios: A future non-Jira backend implementing SupportsLinks for its transport methods would also be required to provide link-translation methods, even if its translation strategy differs from Jira's — the pattern locks translation shape into the capability check.
A reviewer extending the pattern (e.g., adding comment translation) will have nowhere consistent to put it: SupportsComments (transport) vs OutboundMapper (translation).
findings.suggested_fix: Add map_remote_links to InboundMapper (it reads remote link fields → canonical set, exactly as map_remote_to_local reads remote fields → canonical dict) and link_payload_for_relation to OutboundMapper (it translates a canonical relation → vendor payload, exactly as map_fields_to_remote translates canonical fields → vendor fields). SupportsLinks retains only its transport methods. Core guards link diffing with isinstance(backend, SupportsLinks) but calls the translation methods through backend.inbound / backend.outbound.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
